feedback.text: At present, video game publishers have full rights to effectively destroy the game once it has been sold to the customer. This is usually done under the pretext that the game “cannot be supported indefinitely” or similar. It would be useful to add a section obliging gaming-producing companies to take account of the need for aid for the game and to ensure that the game remains available at the end of the aid. This would therefore only ensure that the consumer can continue to use the product for which the full price has already been paid. This idea is already supported by a bigger social movement, Stop Killing Games, and in this context the European Citizens Initiative under the name Stop Destroying Games, which has so far been signed by more than 1.4 million EU citizens. This shows that there is a lot of support for this change.
